grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 Law of Property Act 1925 as an option for modification/discharge
grades.reason: Clearly mentions Upper Tribunal application under s.84 Law of Property Act 1925 with specific grounds

grades.expectation: Response mentions restrictive covenant indemnity insurance with approximate cost range
grades.reason: Provides detailed cost ranges by property value (£200-£800+ premiums) and coverage details

grades.expectation: Response states that approaching the covenant beneficiary invalidates indemnity insurance
grades.reason: Does not mention this critical point about invalidation of insurance if beneficiaries are contacted

grades.expectation: Response assesses risk factors: age of covenant (60yr+), no enforcement history, minimal breach
grades.reason: Specifically addresses all three factors: 60-year-old covenant, no enforcement history, and de minimis breach

grades.expectation: Response mentions Upper Tribunal as the forum for s84 applications
grades.reason: Explicitly states 'Upper Tribunal (Lands Chamber) application under s.84'
